title: Meridian County Micro-Depot Network
strategies: trivial: open the K cheapest candidate sites (cost-only, geometry-blind) and assign every household to its nearest open depot -- reproduces the evaluator's weak reference
greedy: farthest-point sampling (k-center greedy) opens sites that maximize spread, so it covers every district once before ever doubling up inside one -- the textbook single-pass spatial heuristic

strong: adaptive destroy/reinsert search that diagnoses local demand density (k-NN scale) per household, sizes the destroy radius to that density (surgical inside dense pockets, sweeping in the sparse countryside), and uses a threshold-acceptance schedule to escape the coarse-scale local optimum that greedy locks onto
invalid: submits a facility list with duplicate site indices, violating the distinctness contract
